title: One file. All rules. A compact vertical rectangle — the .precept file — with horizontal lines organized into five sections representing the five structural parts of a Precept definition: the precept header, state declarations, event declarations, transition rules, and constraint rules. The single file shape embodies Precept's core guarantee: everything that governs the entity lives in one place, not scattered across services and codebases.
conceptualBridge: The one-file emblem means Precept's governing contract is complete and self-contained because the .precept file contains every state, event, transition, and constraint in one place — the icon IS that file, with every rule visible inside it.
decision: Rejected: 3.69 avg below threshold — too many elements (17 bars + border + fold); visual economy score 3 and monochrome score 3 indicate the design depends too heavily on color differentiation and density. The one-file concept is valid but needs a simpler execution — perhaps just the file shape with 3 bold bars (not 17 fine lines) and a bright highlight.
